Now I can see the button layout more clearly:
Right panel (white background):
- Horizontal bars: rows 41-43, with light gray (w) at cols 37, 41, 45, 49 (4 bars)
- Vertical bars: rows 47-49, with light gray (w) at cols 37, 41, 45, 49 (4 bars)
- Row 45 has "www" patterns between the vertical bars
Left panel (gray background):
- Horizontal bars: rows 41-43, with light gray (w) at cols 9, 13, 17, 21 (4 bars)
- Vertical bars: rows 47-49, with light gray (w) at cols 9, 13, 17, 21 (4 bars)
- Row 45 has "www" patterns between the vertical bars
Wait, looking more carefully:
Right panel horizontal bars (rows 41-43):
- Row 41: WWWWWWWwWWWWwWWWWwWWWWwWWW
  - w at positions 7, 11, 15, 19 (relative to start of line at col 32)
  - So absolute cols: 39, 43, 47, 51
Left panel horizontal bars (rows 41-43):
- Row 41: ggggggwggggwggggwggggwgggg
  - w at positions 7, 11, 15, 19 (relative to start of line at col 2)
  - So absolute cols: 9, 13, 17, 21
Right panel vertical bars (rows 47-49):
- Row 47: WWWWWWWwWWWWwWWWWwWWWWwWWW
  - Same pattern as horizontal bars
Left panel vertical bars (rows 47-49):
- Row 47: ggggggwggggwggggwggggwgggg
  - Same pattern as horizontal bars
So each panel has 4 horizontal bars and 4 vertical bars. The bars are light gray (w) on a white (W) or gray (g) background.
Now, in level 1, the buttons were black (B) and light gray (w), and clicking toggled them. The vertical bars also toggled. The goal was to click the blue blob.
In level 2, the buttons are light gray (w) on white (W) or gray (g) backgrounds. Maybe clicking toggles them between w and W/g?
Let me try clicking on one of the right panel's horizontal bars to see what happens. The first bar is at row 42, col 39 (approximately).
Actually, let me look at the small squares at the bottom more carefully. They have yellow patterns inside:
- Left square (blue bg): yellow at rows 57-59, cols 8-9 (vertical 3x2 rectangle)
- Right square (gray bg): yellow at rows 55-56, cols 20-22 (horizontal 2x3 rectangle)
These might be "target" patterns that I need to recreate using the buttons. Or they might represent the current state of some pattern.
